<current_datetime>2026-05-14T01:42:47.746Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for their `StringFormatter` class. They specifically want to use `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods. However, I should note that in standard MSTest, the string assertion methods are actually `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith` (using the `StringAssert` class, not `Assert`). 

Wait, let me think about this more carefully. In MSTest:
- `StringAssert.StartsWith(string value, string substring)` - checks that value starts with substring
- `StringAssert.EndsWith(string value, string substring)` - checks that value ends with substring


- `StringAssert.Contains(string value, string substring)` - checks that value contains substring

The user specifically asked for `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith`, so I should use those methods as requested, even though the traditional MSTest uses `StringAssert` for these operations. I'll write the tests using the methods they specified.

Writing comprehensive tests for the StringFormatter class...

toolCalls.name: create

data.content:
Here's the test file. A quick note: in MSTest the string-specific assertions use the **`StringAssert`** class, not `Assert`:

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `StringAssert.StartsWith(result, prefix)` | Validates prefix |
| `StringAssert.EndsWith(result, suffix)` | Validates suffix |
| `StringAssert.Contains(result, substring)` | Validates substring presence |

The tests cover all four methods with happy-path format validation and exception cases. `StringAssert` methods give better failure messages than `Assert.IsTrue(result.StartsWith(...))` since they show the actual vs. expected string on failure.
